levels.grammarNote: 是 links subject to noun/identity: 我是学生 (I'm a student). NOT used with adjectives — use 很: 她很漂亮 (she's pretty). NOT used for location — use 在: 他在家 (he's at home).
有 = to have (possession) AND there is/are: 这里有一家咖啡馆 (there's a café here). Negation: 没有 (méiyǒu), never 不有.
的 links modifier to noun: 我的书 (my book), 好的老师 (a good teacher). Adj+的 alone = the [adj] one: 红的 (the red one). Often dropped when close relationship: 我妈 (my mom), 我们学校 (our school).
我 = I (subject) or me (object). 我的 = my. No case distinction in Chinese — same form everywhere.
你 = you (informal). 您 (nín) = polite you (for elders, strangers, customers). 你们 = you (plural). 您们 is rarely used — just 您 suffices even for groups in formal address.
他 = he/him. 她 = she/her. Both pronounced tā — identical in speech! Context makes it clear. 它 (tā) = it (objects/animals). Written differently, sound the same.
我们 = we/us. 咱们 (zánmen) = we/us including the listener (inclusive — Northern dialect). 你们 = you all. 他们/她们 = they.

不 negates most verbs and adjectives: 不好 (not good), 不去 (won't go). Changes to bú before 4th tone: 不是 → bú shì. For 有, use 没 (méi): 没有 (not have). Cannot negate past completed actions — use 没 for that.
了 after a verb = completed action: 我吃了 (I ate). 了 at sentence end = change of state: 他来了！(He's here now!). One of the trickiest particles — it's about completion or change, not just past tense.
在 = to be at/in (location): 她在家 (she's at home). 在+V = progressive (currently doing): 我在学习 (I'm studying). Both uses are very common. Note: 在 never replaces 是 for identity.
说 = to say/speak. 说话 (shuōhuà) = to speak/talk. 说中文 (shuō Zhōngwén) = to speak Chinese. 听说 (tīng shuō) = heard (I heard that).
去 = to go. 去+place: 去北京 (go to Beijing). 去+verb: 去买 (go buy). Compare: 来 (come toward speaker). 走 (zǒu) = to walk OR to leave.
来 = come (toward speaker). Often used where English says 'let me': 我来 (let me do it). 来+verb: 来看看 (come and have a look). 来了！= I'm coming! / Here it is!
要 = to want + noun, OR want to + verb: 我要咖啡 (I want coffee), 我要去 (I want to go). Also signals future: 要下雨了 (it's going to rain). More direct than 想要.
可以 = can (permission or possibility). 我可以进来吗？(May I come in?). 会 = can (skill). 能 (néng) = can (physical ability/circumstance). 可以 is most versatile for asking permission.
会 = can from learning: 我会游泳 (I can swim). Also: will/going to: 明天会下雨 (it will rain tomorrow). 会 implies a learned ability, not just circumstance.
好 before verb = easy to / good to: 好吃 (delicious), 好看 (pretty), 好听 (pleasant-sounding). 好了 = OK / done / all right. 好吗？= Is that OK? Very versatile!
大 = big (size) or great (degree). 大学 (university), 大人 (adult). 太大了 (too big). Opposite: 小 (xiǎo). 大约 (dàyuē) = approximately.

levels.romanized: Zhè shì yī gè hěn dà de chéngshì.
levels.literal: This is one [measure] very big [de] city.

levels.grammarNote: 人 = person (singular) or people (no separate plural form). 中国人 = Chinese person. 人们 (rénmen) = people (general). 有人 = someone. 没人 = nobody.

levels.front: There are a lot of people here.

levels.grammarNote: 时间 = time (in general, uncountable). 时候 (shíhou) = time (a specific point/when). 什么时候 = when. 时间不够 (not enough time). 花时间 (spend time).
知道 = to know facts/information. 认识 (rènshi) = to know people/places (personal acquaintance). 我知道 = I know. 我不知道 = I don't know (extremely common!).
想 = to think (想一想, think about it), to want (想去, want to go), to miss (想你, miss you). 我在想... = I'm thinking about... Very context-dependent!
看 = to look at, watch, read. 看书 (read a book), 看电视 (watch TV), 看医生 (see a doctor). 看一看/看一下 = take a look. 看来 (kànlái) = it seems.
吃 = to eat. 吃饭 (chīfàn) = to eat (lit: eat rice — general term for meals). 吃早饭/午饭/晚饭 = eat breakfast/lunch/dinner. 好吃 = delicious.

levels.front: What do you want to eat?

levels.grammarNote: 喝 = to drink. 喝水 (drink water), 喝茶 (drink tea), 喝酒 (drink alcohol). 喝点什么 = have something to drink.

levels.front: Do you want something to drink?

levels.grammarNote: 做 = to do / make (physical action). 做饭 (cook), 做作业 (do homework), 做生意 (do business). 干 (gàn) is more informal for 'do'. 做 is safe and widely applicable.
买 = to buy. 买东西 = to go shopping (lit: buy things). 买单/买账 = to pay the bill. 卖 (mài, 4th tone) = to sell — same sound, different tone!
钱 = money. 多少钱？ (how much?) is one of the most useful phrases. 块 (kuài) = informal for yuan. 没钱 = broke. 有钱 = wealthy.
多少 = how much (quantity, uncountable) and how many (countable). 多少钱？= how much money? 多少人？= how many people? No measure word needed between 多少 and the noun.
什么 = what. 你叫什么名字？(What's your name?). 什么时候 (when), 什么地方 (where), 什么人 (who). 没什么 = nothing / it's nothing.
哪里 (nǎlǐ) = where (standard Mandarin). 哪儿 (nǎr) = where (Beijing/Northern dialect, common in speech). 在哪里？= where is it? 你是哪里人？= where are you from?
为什么 = why (asking for a reason). 因为 (yīnwèi) = because. 为什么+verb: 你为什么不去？(Why aren't you going?)
怎么 = how (manner/method): 怎么去？(how to get there?). Also 'why' when surprised: 你怎么来了？(Why are you here?). 怎么了 = what's wrong? 怎么样 = how is it?
谁 = who. Two accepted pronunciations: shéi and shuí. 那是谁？(Who is that?). 谁知道！= Who knows! 谁都知道 = everyone knows.
这 = this. 这个 (zhège) = this one (with measure word 个). 这里/这儿 = here. 这样 = like this. 这么 = so/this (degree): 这么好 (this good / so good).
那 = that. 那个 (nàge) = that one. 那里/那儿 = there. 那么 = so/then: 那么好 (so good). 那 at sentence start = in that case / then.

很 is required between subject and adjective predicate, but adds little emphasis — it's often just a filler: 我很好 (lit: I very good = I'm fine). For emphasis use 非常 (fēicháng) = extremely, or 太...了 = too/so.

levels.romanized: Tā Zhōngwén shuō de hěn hǎo.

levels.grammarNote: 也 = also/too. Always before the verb: 我也去 (I'm going too). Not at the end of a sentence like English! 也好 = also good / fine.
都 = all/both (adverb, placed before verb). 大家都来了 (everyone came). 我们都是学生 (we're all students). 都 must follow the noun phrase it refers to.
还 = still (continuing): 你还在吗？(Are you still there?). Also = as well: 我还要一个 (I'll have another one too). 还是 = or (in questions), or still/rather.
就 is incredibly versatile: emphasis (就是, it's exactly), immediacy (我就来, I'll be right there), logical result (那就去吧, then go), only/as few as (就一个, just one).
只 = only (adverb before verb). 只有一个 (only one). 只是 = it's just that. 只要 = as long as / just need to. Similar to 就 but specifically limits.
已经 = already (usually before a verb). 我已经吃了 (I've already eaten). Often paired with 了 for completed actions. 已经太晚了 (it's already too late).
没有 = not have AND there isn't/aren't. 没有时间 (no time). 没有人 (nobody). Shortened to 没 before a verb: 我没去 (I didn't go). 没有 is the negation of both 有 and past actions.
一起 = together (adverb before verb). 我们一起去 (let's go together). 一起来 (come together). Very common in invitations.
现在 = now. 现在几点？(What time is it now?). 从现在开始 = starting from now. 就现在 = right now.
今天 = today. 今天几号？(What date is today?). 今天怎么样？(How was today?). 今天 at start of sentence gives time context.
明天 = tomorrow. 后天 (hòutiān) = day after tomorrow. 昨天 (zuótiān) = yesterday. 前天 (qiántiān) = day before yesterday.  明天见！= See you tomorrow!
一点 = a little bit (degree, before adjective or after verb). 一点儿 = slightly more colloquial (Beijing). 有一点 + adj = a bit [adj]: 有一点贵 (a bit expensive). 点 alone after a verb = a bit.
对 = correct/right (对！= That's right!). 对+person = toward: 对我说 (say to me). 对不起 (duìbuqǐ) = sorry. 不对 = wrong. 对了 = by the way! (also 'that's it!')
什么时候 = when (asking time). 你什么时候来？(When are you coming?). 到时候 = when the time comes. 有时候 = sometimes.
多 = many/much (quantity) or how (in questions): 多高？(how tall?), 多少？(how many?). 很多 = a lot of. 多了 = many / too many. 多吃点 = eat more.

levels.front: There are a lot of people.

levels.grammarNote: 少 = few/little (quantity). Opposite of 多. 少一点 = a little less. 至少 (zhìshǎo) = at least. 不少 = quite a lot (double negative).

levels.front: Could I have a little less?

levels.grammarNote: 喜欢 = to like. 喜欢+noun: 我喜欢音乐 (I like music). 喜欢+verb: 我喜欢唱歌 (I like singing). More casual than 爱. 不喜欢 = don't like.
爱 = to love (strong feeling — people, activities you're passionate about). 我爱你 = I love you. 爱好 (àihào) = hobby. Less casual than 喜欢.
吗 at sentence end turns a statement into a yes/no question: 你好吗？(Are you well?). Never used with question words like 什么, 哪里 — those already make questions.
呢 softens questions or asks 'what about?'. 你呢？= And you? / What about you? Can also show ongoing action: 她在睡觉呢 (she's sleeping right now).
吧 makes suggestions (我们去吧 = let's go) or seeks agreement like 'right?': 你是学生吧？(You're a student, right?). Softens commands. Very frequent in spoken Chinese.
再 = again (for future repeated action): 再来一次 (do it once more). 再见 (zàijiàn) = goodbye (see you again). Compare: 又 (yòu) = again (already happened).
先 = first / before (order of actions). 你先去，我后来 (you go first, I'll come later). 先...然后... = first... then... 先生 (xiānsheng) = Mr. / husband (different use!).
然后 = then / after that (sequencing). 先...然后... = first... then.... 我先吃，然后再去 (I'll eat first, then go).
但是 = but/however (formal). 可是 = but (more conversational). 虽然...但是 = although...but. Both followed by the contrasting clause.

levels.front: I want to go but I'm busy.
levels.romanized: Wǒ xiǎng qù, dànshì wǒ hěn máng.
levels.literal: I want go, but I very busy.

levels.grammarNote: 因为 = because. 因为...所以... = because... therefore... (often paired). 因为我忙，所以没来 (because I was busy, I didn't come). Can stand alone: 为什么？—因为我累了。
所以 = therefore/so (result). 因为...所以... = because... so... Stands alone at start of clause: 所以我没去 (so I didn't go). Very common in spoken Chinese.
如果 = if. 如果...的话 = if... (adding 的话 is optional but very common). 如果你来，我很高兴 (if you come, I'd be happy). 要是 (yàoshi) = if (more informal).

levels.back: 如果你有空，我们见个面吧。
levels.romanized: Rúguǒ nǐ yǒu kòng, wǒmen jiàn gè miàn ba.
levels.literal: If you have free-time, we meet one-measure face [suggestion].

levels.grammarNote: 虽然 = although. Always paired with 但是/可是/还是: 虽然很贵，但是很好 (although it's expensive, it's good). 虽然 MUST have a contrast — can't stand alone.

levels.romanized: Suīrán hěn guì, dànshì hěn hǎo.
levels.literal: Although very expensive, but very good.

levels.grammarNote: 而且 = and moreover/furthermore (adding information). 它便宜，而且好吃 (it's cheap and moreover delicious). More emphatic than just 和 (and).

levels.front: It's cheap and also very tasty.

levels.grammarNote: 1一 2二 3三 4四 5五 6六 7七 8八 9九 10十. 两 (liǎng) is used for 'two' before measure words: 两个 (two [of them]). 二 (èr) for numbers: 第二 (second), 二十 (twenty).
11十一, 12十二, 20二十, 21二十一, 30三十, 50五十, 99九十九, 100一百. Pattern: tens+ones. Very regular! 百 (bǎi) = hundred.
1,000 = 一千, 10,000 = 一万 (wàn — key unit!), 100,000 = 十万, 1,000,000 = 一百万. Chinese groups by 万 (10k), not 1,000 like English. 一亿 (yīyì) = 100 million.
上 = up/above (spatial), previous (上个月 = last month), to go up. 上班 = to go to work. 马上 (mǎshàng) = immediately. 上网 = to go online. Very productive character!
下 = below/down (spatial), next (下个星期 = next week), to go down. 下班 = to finish work. 下课 = class is over. 下雨 = to rain. 以下 (yǐxià) = below/following.
前 = front (spatial), before (time): 以前 (before/in the past). 前面 = in front. 三天前 = three days ago. 前天 = day before yesterday. 向前走 = go forward.

levels.front: In the past, it was different.

levels.grammarNote: 后 = back/behind (spatial), after (time): 以后 (after/in the future), 后天 (day after tomorrow). 后面 = behind. 然后 = then/after that. 以后 = in the future / from now on.
里 = inside / in. 在里面 = inside. 家里 = at home (in the house). 哪里 = where. Always after the noun: 书里 (in the book), 心里 (in one's heart).
东西 = thing/stuff (neutral tone on 西). 你要什么东西？= What do you want? 买东西 = to shop (buy things). Note: 东西 literally means east-west — direction-derived word!
地方 = place/area/spot. 在什么地方？= where? 有个好地方 = there's a great place. 地方 is more general than specific locations.

levels.front: Do you know a good place?
levels.romanized: Nǐ zhīdào shénme hǎo dìfang ma?

levels.grammarNote: 事情 = matter/thing/affair (abstract). 有事情 = have something on / be busy. 没事 (méishì) = nothing/never mind. 什么事？= What's the matter?
问题 = question AND problem. 有问题吗？= Any questions? / Is there a problem? 没问题 = no problem! 解决问题 = to solve a problem.
意思 = meaning OR intention. 什么意思？= what does that mean? 你是什么意思？= what do you mean? 有意思 = interesting. 没意思 = boring.
关系 = relationship, connection, and the famous concept of 'guanxi' (social networks/connections). 没关系 = it doesn't matter / that's OK. 有关系 = it matters / related.
方法 = method/way/approach. 有什么方法？= Is there a way? 用这个方法 = using this method. 好方法！= Great idea/approach!

levels.romanized: Yǒu méiyǒu gèng hǎo de fāngfǎ?

levels.grammarNote: 情况 = situation/condition/circumstances. 什么情况？= What's the situation? / What's going on? (very colloquial). 根据情况 = depending on the situation.
数字 = number (numeral/digit). 号码 (hàomǎ) = number (ID number, phone number, room number). 手机号码 = phone number. 编号 = serial number.

levels.romanized: Nǐ de shǒujī hàomǎ shì duōshao?

levels.grammarNote: 分钟 = minute(s). 五分钟 = 5 minutes. 半个小时 = half an hour. 等一分钟 = wait a minute. 分 alone works in telling time: 三点五分 (3:05).
小时 = hour (duration): 两个小时 (two hours). 点 = o'clock (time): 三点 (3 o'clock). 现在几点？= What time is it? 几点 = what time.
天 = day (informal, very common): 今天 (today), 明天 (tomorrow), 三天 (3 days). 日 = day (more formal/written): 生日 (birthday), 节日 (holiday).
星期 = week (spoken). 周 = week (formal/written, common in business). 星期一 = Monday. 下个星期 = next week. 上个星期 = last week. 星期天/日 = Sunday.
年 = year. 今年 (jīnnián) = this year. 去年 (qùnián) = last year. 明年 (míngnián) = next year. 年龄 (niánlíng) = age. 每年 (měi nián) = every year.
月 = month AND moon. 这个月 = this month. 上个月 = last month. Months: 一月 (January) through 十二月 (December). 月亮 (yuèliang) = the moon.

levels.romanized: Nǐ de shēngrì shì jǐ yuè?

levels.grammarNote: 左 = left. 右 = right. 向左/右转 = turn left/right. 左边 = left side. 右边 = right side. 往左走 = go to the left.

levels.front: Turn left at the next corner.
levels.romanized: Zài qiánmiàn de lùkǒu xiàng zuǒ zhuǎn.
levels.literal: At front [de] intersection toward left turn.

levels.grammarNote: 国家 = country/nation. 中国 = China. 美国 = America/USA. 英国 = UK. Pattern: [place]+国 = that country. 外国 = foreign country.

levels.romanized: Nǐ shì nǎ gè guójiā de rén?

levels.grammarNote: 语言 = language (formal). 中文 = written Chinese. 普通话 = Mandarin (standard spoken). 广东话 = Cantonese. 说...话 = to speak [language].
明白 = understand (grasp clearly). 懂 = understand (comprehend). 我听不懂 = I can't understand (listening). 我不明白 = I don't get it. Both very common.
需要 = to need (necessity). 需要+noun or +verb: 我需要帮助 (I need help). More about genuine need vs 要 which can be more 'want'.
帮助 = to help (formal noun/verb). 帮 = to help (informal verb). 帮我 = help me. 需要帮忙吗？(Do you need help?) 帮忙 (bāngmáng) = to help out.
觉得 = to think/feel (expressing opinion or sensation). 我觉得... = I think/feel... More personal than 认为 (rènwéi = to think/believe formally).
希望 = to hope/wish. 我希望你能来 (I hope you can come). 希望 as noun = hope. 希望如此 = hopefully so.

levels.front: I hope to see you again.

levels.grammarNote: 等 = to wait. 等一下 (děng yīxià) = wait a moment. 等等！= Wait! 我等你 = I'll wait for you. 等不及 = can't wait (excited).
对不起 = sorry (for something serious). 不好意思 = sorry/excuse me (embarrassed, for minor things). 没关系 = that's OK (response to apology). 抱歉 (bàoqiàn) = sorry (formal).

levels.romanized: Qǐng xiě xià nǐ de míngzì.

levels.grammarNote: Also pronounced dòu in some contexts
Can function as verb or preposition

levels.back: to open / to drive / to start
levels.grammarNote: Context determines meaning

levels.back: to put / to place / to release
Put the book on the table.
levels.romanized: Bǎ shū fàng zài zhuōzi shàng.
levels.literal: Put book place on table top

levels.back: to bring / to carry / to wear

levels.grammarNote: Used as direction or time indicator
Very common, often used as directional complement

levels.back: to hit / to make (a call) / to play (ball sports)
to send / to deliver / to give as a gift
to receive / to pick up / to connect

levels.romanized: Wǒ kěyǐ wèn yīgè wèntí ma?

levels.back: Please tell me how to do it.
to pay attention / to be careful

levels.grammarNote: Informal; 父亲 (fùqīn) is formal
Informal; 母亲 (mǔqīn) is formal
Chinese distinguishes older/younger siblings

levels.back: My older brother is 25 years old.
My younger brother is still in school.
My younger sister is very smart.
Grandfather is 70 years old this year.

levels.romanized: Wǒ nǎinai zuòfàn hěn hǎo chī.

levels.grammarNote: Used in many regions; 姥爷 (lǎoye) in North China

levels.back: Maternal grandfather likes to play chess.

levels.grammarNote: Used in many regions; 姥姥 (lǎolao) in North China

levels.back: Maternal grandmother lives in the countryside.
My son is five years old.
How many children do you have?

levels.romanized: Tā shì wǒ zuì hǎo de péngyou.
Tā de nǚ péngyou shì Rìběn rén.

levels.back: I eat together with my colleagues.

levels.grammarNote: Chinese has different words for different uncle/aunt relationships

levels.back: Uncle came to my house for dinner.

levels.grammarNote: Also used as polite address for any middle-aged woman

levels.romanized: Āyí gěi wǒ dài le lǐwù.

levels.back: We visit relatives during Chinese New Year.

levels.grammarNote: Different from 知道 which is to know a fact

levels.back: to get sick / to be ill
I need to see a doctor.
What time do you go to sleep?

levels.grammarNote: Less offensive in Chinese than in English

levels.back: I have gained a little weight recently.

levels.grammarNote: Note: 眼睛 (yǎnjīng) is eyes, 眼镜 (yǎnjìng) is glasses
Can refer to rice specifically or meals generally

levels.back: I ate two steamed buns for breakfast.
levels.romanized: Zǎocān wǒ chī le liǎng gè bāozi.

levels.back: Eating dumplings during Spring Festival is a tradition.
I want a bowl of rice.
I eat bread in the morning.
The beef here is very delicious.
levels.romanized: Zhèlǐ de niúròu hěn hǎo chī.

levels.back: Eating more vegetables is good for your health.
levels.romanized: Duō chī shūcài duì shēntǐ hǎo.

levels.back: Do you drink tea or coffee?

levels.grammarNote: Loanword from English

levels.back: My child drinks milk every day.
I want a glass of orange juice.
levels.romanized: Wǒ yào yī bēi chéng zhī.

levels.grammarNote: Literally "good eat"

levels.romanized: Wǒmen kěyǐ diǎn cài le ma?

levels.grammarNote: Also 结账 (jiézhàng)

levels.back: We watch TV in the living room.
Mom is cooking in the kitchen.
My bedroom is on the second floor.
levels.romanized: Wǒ de wòshì zài èr lóu.

levels.grammarNote: Also 厕所 (cèsuǒ) or 洗手间 (xǐshǒujiān)

levels.back: The book is on the table.
Take the stairs or the elevator?
Please turn on the air conditioner.

levels.romanized: Zhè tiáo kùzi tài dà le.

levels.back: She is wearing a red skirt.
levels.romanized: Tā chuān le yī tiáo hóng qúnzi.

levels.back: It's cold, put on your coat.
levels.romanized: Tiān lěng le, chuān shàng wàitào.

levels.back: Wear a hat to protect from the sun.
You need to wear gloves in winter.
She is wearing a red scarf.
levels.romanized: Tā dài le yī tiáo hóng wéijīn.

levels.grammarNote: Used for clothes, shoes, and socks

levels.back: What shoe size do you wear?

levels.grammarNote: Used for hats, glasses, jewelry, watches

levels.romanized: Zhè jiàn yīfu dàxiǎo héshì ma?

levels.back: I am going to the supermarket to buy things.
I need to go to the bank to withdraw money.
levels.romanized: Wǒ yào qù yínháng qǔ qián.

levels.back: The post office is next to the bank.

levels.front: 我在一家外国公司工作。
levels.back: I work at a foreign company.
levels.romanized: Wǒ zài yī jiā wàiguó gōngsī gōngzuò.

levels.back: What time do we arrive at the airport?
How do I get to the train station?

levels.grammarNote: Often shortened to 公交车 (gōngjiāo chē)

levels.back: Which bus route do I take?

levels.grammarNote: Also called 的士 (dīshì) in some regions

levels.romanized: Wǒ dǎ le yī liàng chūzū chē.

levels.back: How long does it take by plane?
levels.romanized: Zuò fēijī yào duō cháng shíjiān?

levels.back: I am taking the train to Beijing.

Petroleum is an important energy source.
Science and technology have changed life.
Artificial intelligence is developing very quickly.

levels.front: 大数据改变了商业模式。
levels.back: Big data has changed business models.
levels.romanized: Dà shùjù gǎibiàn le shāngyè móshì.

levels.back: Cloud computing makes work more convenient.
levels.romanized: Yún jìsuàn ràng gōngzuò gèng fāngbiàn.

levels.back: Innovation is the driving force of development.
I especially like the food you cook.
levels.romanized: Wǒ tèbié xǐhuān nǐ zuò de cài.

levels.back: This is just an ordinary question.
I have been waiting for you all along.
It is just a minor problem.

levels.romanized: Qǐng gěi yīgè zhǔnquè de dá'àn.

levels.back: The data needs to be precise.

levels.romanized: Zhè shì yīgè diǎnxíng de lìzi.

levels.back: You must be careful in what you do.

levels.grammarNote: Add 第 before numbers for ordinals

levels.back: He was the last one to arrive.
levels.romanized: Tā shì zuìhòu yīgè dào de.

levels.back: It takes at least an hour.
His opinion is opposite to mine.
levels.romanized: Tā de yìjiàn hé wǒ xiāngfǎn.

levels.back: The number of people exceeded one thousand.
The temperature is below zero degrees.
The price is higher than expected.

levels.grammarNote: Literally "left-right"; used for rough estimates

levels.back: Brush your teeth morning and night.
levels.romanized: Zǎo wǎn dōu yào shuā yá.

levels.back: I take a shower every day.
Comb your hair before going out.
She puts on makeup every day.
He is looking in the mirror.
to get up out of bed
What time do you get up?
I need to use the restroom.
Drinking more water is good for your health.
levels.romanized: Duō hē shuǐ duì shēntǐ hǎo.

levels.back: to take a walk / to stroll
We take a walk after dinner.
Greet your neighbors when you see them.

levels.romanized: Tā gěi le wǒ yīgè yōngbào.
Tā yǒu yīgè měilì de wēixiào.
Tā tàn le yī kǒu qì.

levels.back: He shook his head to decline.
originally / as a matter of fact
as a result / in the end
levels.grammarNote: Can be noun (result) or conjunction (as a result)

levels.back: As a result, he was late.
in the end / when all is said and done

levels.front: 说来说去还是这个问题。
levels.back: When all is said and done, it comes back to this problem.
levels.romanized: Shuō lái shuō qù háishi zhège wèntí.

levels.back: Where was I at that time?
At the same time, they were preparing.
levels.romanized: Yǔ cǐ tóngshí, tāmen zài zhǔnbèi.

levels.back: Generally speaking, the results are good.
in fact / as a matter of fact
In fact, this is not true.
levels.romanized: Shìshí shàng, zhè bùshì zhēn de.

levels.front: 相对而言，这个方法更好。
levels.back: Relatively speaking, this method is better.
levels.romanized: Xiāngduì ér yán, zhège fāngfǎ gèng hǎo.

levels.front: 根据天气预报，明天下雨。
levels.back: According to the weather forecast, it will rain tomorrow.
levels.romanized: Gēnjù tiānqì yùbào, míngtiān xià yǔ.

levels.back: What is the basis for this?

levels.front: 关于这件事，我有个问题。
levels.back: Regarding this matter, I have a question.
levels.romanized: Guānyú zhè jiàn shì, wǒ yǒu gè wèntí.

levels.back: aimed at / directed at / with regard to
This policy is aimed at young people.
through / by means of / to pass

levels.front: 通过努力，我通过了考试。
levels.back: Through hard work, I passed the exam.
levels.romanized: Tōngguò nǔlì, wǒ tōngguò le kǎoshì.

levels.back: to use / to make use of
Make use of free time to study.

levels.romanized: Wǒ shíxiàn le wǒ de mèngxiǎng.

levels.front: 我们需要完善这个方案。
levels.back: We need to improve this plan.
to improve / to raise / to enhance

levels.front: 我要提高我的中文水平。
levels.back: I want to improve my Chinese level.
levels.romanized: Wǒ yào tígāo wǒ de zhōngwén shuǐpíng.
Wǒmen jiànlì le xīn de xìtǒng.

levels.back: Maintaining the status quo is not easy.

levels.front: 我们要探索新的可能性。
levels.romanized: Wǒmen yào tànsuǒ xīn de kěnéngxìng.

levels.back: We need to analyze the data.
We need to evaluate the risks.

levels.front: 这里距离北京三百公里。
levels.back: This place is 300 kilometers from Beijing.

levels.romanized: Zhè kē shù yǒu shí mǐ gāo.

levels.back: Please give me one liter of water.
levels.romanized: Qǐng gěi wǒ yī shēng shuǐ.

levels.front: 这个房间有二十平方米。
levels.romanized: Zhège fángjiān yǒu èrshí píngfāng mǐ.

levels.grammarNote: Interjection expressing surprise or disappointment
Used when answering phone or getting attention
Sentence-final or exclamatory particle

levels.back: Oh, so that is how it is!

levels.grammarNote: Indicates realization or understanding
Expresses disappointment or resignation
Affirmative or thoughtful response

levels.back: What a pity you cannot come.
best / it would be best to

levels.romanized: Bùxìng de shì, tā shībài le.

levels.back: Just in case it rains, bring an umbrella.
levels.romanized: Wànyī xià yǔ, dài bǎ sǎn.

levels.back: I meditate for ten minutes every day.

levels.romanized: Tā xué le shí nián wǔshù.

levels.back: He is passionate about public welfare.
He donated money to a charity.
levels.romanized: Tā juān kuǎn gěi císhàn jīgòu.

levels.back: We should cooperate for mutual benefit.

levels.grammarNote: China's major international development strategy

levels.front: 一带一路促进了国际合作。
levels.back: The Belt and Road Initiative has promoted international cooperation.
levels.romanized: Yīdài Yīlù cùjìn le guójì hézuò.

levels.grammarNote: China's economic policy since 1978

levels.front: 改革开放让中国快速发展。
levels.back: Reform and opening-up enabled China to develop rapidly.
levels.romanized: Gǎigé kāifàng ràng Zhōngguó kuàisù fāzhǎn.

levels.grammarNote: Political term: a society of moderate prosperity

levels.front: 中国实现了全面小康社会。
levels.back: China has achieved a moderately prosperous society.
levels.romanized: Zhōngguó shíxiàn le quánmiàn xiǎokāng shèhuì.

levels.back: China's high-speed rail is very developed.

levels.back: He is live streaming to sell products.
Digital payment is becoming increasingly common.
levels.romanized: Shùzì zhīfù yuè lái yuè pǔbiàn.
Qūkuài liàn jìshù hěn yǒu qiántú.

levels.front: 元宇宙是新的互联网形态。
levels.back: The metaverse is a new form of the internet.
levels.romanized: Yuányǔzhòu shì xīn de hùliánwǎng xíngtài.

levels.front: 可持续发展是全球目标。
levels.back: Sustainable development is a global goal.
levels.romanized: Kě chíxù fāzhǎn shì quánqiú mùbiāo.

levels.back: China has pledged to achieve carbon neutrality.

levels.grammarNote: Major political achievement in China

levels.front: 中国完成了脱贫攻坚任务。
levels.back: China has completed its poverty alleviation task.
levels.romanized: Zhōngguó wánchéng le tuōpín gōngjiān rènwù.

levels.grammarNote: Important political and governance concept

levels.front: 以人为本是重要的理念。
levels.back: People-centered governance is an important concept.
levels.romanized: Yǐ rén wéi běn shì zhòngyào de lǐniàn.

levels.back: to keep pace with the times

levels.back: We must keep pace with the times.

levels.back: Practice brings knowledge; do more experiments.

huó dào lǎo xué dào lǎo

levels.front: 活到老学到老，不断进步。
levels.back: Never too old to learn; keep improving.

levels.back: everyone is responsible for their country's fate
levels.romanized: tiānxià xīng wáng pǐfū yǒu zé
levels.grammarNote: Famous historical quote by Gu Yanwu

levels.back: Every citizen bears responsibility for the nation.
levels.romanized: Tiānxià xīng wáng pǐfū yǒu zé.

levels.back: the road ahead is long and hard
levels.romanized: lù màn màn qí xiū yuǎn xī
levels.grammarNote: Opening line from Qu Yuan's poem "Li Sao"

levels.front: 路漫漫其修远兮，吾将上下而求索。
levels.back: The road is long and hard; I shall seek high and low.
levels.romanized: Lù màn màn qí xiū yuǎn xī, wú jiāng shàngxià ér qiú suǒ.
